metadata.name: 16.A.1 Number of foundations, associations and social organizations recorded in the REGON register per 10 thous. population
metadata.goal: Goal 16. Peace and justice - strong institutions
metadata.priority: Combating social and economic exclusion at the institutional level by building modern decision-making mechanisms, social and economic participation at all levels
metadata.definition: The number of entities of the national economy - foundations, associations and social organizations recorded in the REGON register per 10000 inhabitants.
metadata.methodology: A foundation – a legal person whose activity is based on assets transferred by a founder and on current payments (subsidies), which is established for realization of socially or economically useful purposes compatible with the fundamental interests of the Republic of Poland, in particular such as: health care, development of economy and science, education, culture and art, human health and social work activities, environmental protection and monument preservation. Foundation acts under the Law of 6 April 1984 on foundations and on the basis of its statute. It acquires legal entity with being recorded in the National Court Register.A founder – a natural person irrespective of their nationality and place of residence, or a legal person with its seat in Poland or abroad.An association – a voluntary, self-governing, stable union of people for non-profit purposes, which independently determines its goals, activity programs and organizational structures, as well as adopts internal rules regarding its activities. Polish citizens with full legal capacity and public rights have the right to form associations. The legal basis for activities is the Law of 7 April 1989 on associations and an adopted statute. An association acquires legal entity and can start its activities with being recorded in the National Court Register. A simplified form of association is an ordinary association which does not have legal entity.REGON (National Official Register of Economic Entities) is a set of information on entities of national economy run in the computer system in the form of central and local databases. Currently, it is the only integrated register in Poland which involves all entities of national economy. Every entity has a unique nine-digit identification number (REGON) given by the Central Statistical Office, which is used in banking, customs, tax systems as well as in processing of data on foreign trade and in other commercial and marketing systems.According to the Act of 29 June 1995 on public statistics, every entity of the national economy is required to register its business activity in the REGON.

metadata.name: 16.A.2 Voter turnout in local government elections (first round)
metadata.goal: Goal 16. Peace and justice - strong institutions
metadata.priority: Combating social and economic exclusion at the institutional level by building modern decision-making mechanisms, social and economic participation at all levels
metadata.definition: Ratio of the number of votes cast to the total number of persons entitled to vote in the first round of elections for the positions of mayors of villages, towns and cities.
metadata.methodology: Voter turnout - ratio of the number of votes cast (valid ballots) to the total number of persons entitled to vote.Entitled to vote - A person included in the list of voters whose voting rights are particularized in the art. 10 of the Election Code.Village mayor (mayor, and president of a city) - single-person executive body of municipalities. Village mayor is the executive body of a rural municipalities. The mayor is the executive body of municipalities in which the seat of government is located in the city that lies in the territory of municipalities. President of a city is the executive body in cities with more than 100 thousand residents. This also applies to cities in which, until the Act of 8 March 1990 municipalities local government entered into force, the president of a city was the executive-managing body.
metadata.availability: Data available according to the electoral cycle; since 2014
metadata.notes: In 1998 -2018, local elections were held every four years. In 2018, the term of office of local government bodies was extended to 5 years.

metadata.name: 16.B.1 Detection rate of ascertained crimes by the Police in completed preparatory proceedings
metadata.goal: Goal 16. Peace and justice - strong institutions
metadata.priority: Security and development of security management systems that increase the adaptive capacity and reduce threats to the region
metadata.definition: Relation of the number of offences detected in a given year (including those detected after being discharged) to the number of offences established in a given year, increased by the number of offences established in the proceedings undertaken and discontinued in previous years due to the non-detection of perpetrators.
metadata.methodology: Determined crime - an event that has been confirmed as a crime in the completed pre-trial proceedings.Detected crime - an offence established in a completed pre-trial investigation in which at least one suspect has been identified.
metadata.source: Ministry of Justice / National Prosecutors Office / Statistics Poland

metadata.name: 16.B.2 Events requiring the participation of fire protection units per 1 thous. population
metadata.goal: Goal 16. Peace and justice - strong institutions
metadata.priority: Security and development of security management systems that increase the adaptive capacity and reduce threats to the region
metadata.definition: Total number of fires, local hazards and false alarms per 1000 population (as of 30 June of the surveyed  year).
metadata.methodology: Fire - an uncontrolled burning process in a place not intended for this purpose, resulting in material and ecological losses. Local threat - other than fire incidents resulting from the development of civilization, human activities or natural forces of nature, posing a threat to life, health, property or the environment.False alarm - a fire alarm reported under the circumstances when there is no fire, there was no fire and there was no reason to bring on the fire.
metadata.source: Statistics Poland / Ministry of the Interior and Administration

metadata.name: 16.B.3 Total expenditures of gminas and powiats on public safety and fire protection per capita
metadata.goal: Goal 16. Peace and justice - strong institutions
metadata.priority: Security and development of security management systems that increase the adaptive capacity and reduce threats to the region
metadata.definition: Total expenditure of gminas and powiats dedicated to public safety and fire protection (i.e.  expenditure in Division 754 of budgetary classification) - per capita.
metadata.methodology: Information on expenditure of self government entities is based on reports  of Ministry of Finance.The following categories are included in local self-government entities: gminas (excluding gminas possessing city status with powiat status,) cities with powiat status, powiats, voivodships.Budgetary expenses of local self-government entities are grouped according to the principles set forth in the Act of Public Finances, Act on Gmina Self-government, Act on Powiat Self-government, Act on Voivodship and Self-government and Local Government Finance Act. The expenses are grouped by divisions and chapters (defining types of activity) and paragraphs (defining types of expenditure). Detailed classification of expenditure is determined by the Minister of Finance under the subsequent regulations.Among types of expenditure, current expenditure and property expenditure (including investments) can be distinguished. Current expenditure includes allocations, benefits for natural person, current expenditure of budgetary entities (including wages and salaries, wages and salaries related expenditures, purchase of goods and services).Among kinds of activities, according to divisions and chapters of the budgetary classification - among others, public safety and fire protection (Division 754) is identifiied.

metadata.name: 16.C.1 Voters on projects for 10 thous. inhabitants
metadata.goal: Goal 16. Peace and justice - strong institutions
metadata.priority: Development and strengthening of state institutions and organizations at all levels in the region
metadata.definition: Number of inhabitants who took part in voting on projects submitted in the form of a civic budget per 10 000 population (as of 30 June of the surveyed  year).
metadata.methodology: Civic budget (otherwise: participatory budget) – Financial resources separated from the municipalities budget and committed for projects drawn in the process of open submission of ideas, public debate and voting, conducted during public consultation. In municipalities being with cities with counties status, the creation of a civic budget is mandatory.Public consultation – activities undertaken by local authorities, including informing and then hearing the views of residents or organisations representing different social groups on planned decisions, in order to find optimal solutions. Consultations take place in cases provided for by law (mandatory consultations) and in other matters important for the municipality. The rules and procedure for conducting public consultations should be specified in the resolution of the municipalities council.

metadata.name: 16.C.2 Investment expenditure of local government units per capita
metadata.goal: Goal 16. Peace and justice - strong institutions
metadata.priority: Development and strengthening of state institutions and organizations at all levels in the region
metadata.definition: The sum of capital expenditures of local government units incurred in a given year per capita (as of 30 June of the surveyed year).
metadata.methodology: Investment outlays - financial or tangible outlays, the purpose of which is the creation of new fixed assets or the improvement (rebuilding, enlargement, reconstruction, modernization) of existing capital asset items, as well as outlays on so-called initial investments. Investment outlays are divided into outlays on fixed assets and other outlays. Outlays on fixed assets include outlays on:• buildings and structures (including buildings and places as well as civil engineering works) of which, among others construction and assembly,• works, design and cost estimate documentations,• machinery, technical equipment and tools (including instruments, moveables and endowments),• transport equipment,• others, i.e., detailed meliorations, costs incurred for purchasing land and second-hand fixed assets, livestock (basic herd) and long-term plantings, interests on investment credits and investment loans for the period of investment realization.Other outlays are outlays on so-called initial investments as well as other costs connected with investment realization. These outlays do not increase the value of fixed assets.

metadata.name: 16.C.3 Share of local government bodies investment expenditure in total expenditure
metadata.goal: Goal 16. Peace and justice - strong institutions
metadata.priority: Development and strengthening of state institutions and organizations at all levels in the region
metadata.definition: Relation of the sum of capital expenditures of local government units to the sum of their total expenditures incurred in a given year.
metadata.methodology: Investment outlays - financial or tangible outlays, the purpose of which is the creation of new fixed assets or the improvement (rebuilding, enlargement, reconstruction, modernization) of existing capital asset items, as well as outlays on so-called initial investments. Investment outlays are divided into outlays on fixed assets and other outlays.Outlays on fixed assets include outlays on:• buildings and structures (including buildings and places as well as civil engineering works) of which, among others construction and assembly,• works, design and cost estimate documentations,• machinery, technical equipment and tools (including instruments, moveables and endowments),• transport equipment,• others, i.e., detailed meliorations, costs incurred for purchasing land and second-hand fixed assets, livestock (basic herd) and long-term plantings, interests on investment credits and investment loans for the period of investment realization.Other outlays are outlays on so-called initial investments as well as other costs connected with investment realization. These outlays do not increase the value of fixed assets.Expenditures of budgets of local government units - consist of current expenditures and property expenditures. By the current expenditures of the budget of the local government unit are meant the budgetary expenditures not being property expenditures. Among current expenditures stands out in particular: expenditures of budgetary units for wages and salaries and related to them expenditures and for the statutory activity of these units; grants for current tasks; benefits for natural persons; payments for guarantees granted by the local government unit, due for repayment in a given year; the local government unit's debt servicing; expenditures for programmes financed with a participation of the funds referred to in art. 5 pra. 1 point 2 and 3 of the Public Finance Act, in the part related to the realization of local government unit's tasks. Property expenditures include expenditures for: investments and investment purchases, of which for programmes financed with a participation of the funds referred to in art. 5 pra. 1 point 2 and 3 of the Public Finance Act, in the part related to the realization of local government unit's tasks; a purchase and subscription of stocks and shares; a contribution to commercial law companies.

metadata.name: 16.C.4 Funds from EU obtained by local government bodies to finance programs and projects EU per capita
metadata.goal: Goal 16. Peace and justice - strong institutions
metadata.priority: Development and strengthening of state institutions and organizations at all levels in the region
metadata.definition: Total funds for financing programmes and projects received from the European Union by local government units per capita (as of 30 June of  the surveyed  year).
metadata.methodology: Data concern the funds obtained by local governments of voivodships, powiats, municipalities and cities with counties rights.

metadata.name: 16.C.5 Share of area covered by local-land development plans in urban gminas
metadata.goal: Goal 16. Peace and justice - strong institutions
metadata.priority: Development and strengthening of state institutions and organizations at all levels in the region
metadata.definition: Share of urban gminas’ area covered by applicable local spatial management plans in the total geodetic area in urban gminas.
metadata.methodology: Local spatial management plan is an act of local law determining destiny, terms of management and development of land, as well as arrangement of public investments. The plan consists of a description that is a text of the plan contained in resolution of the City Council and the drawing of the plan that is a graphical annex to the resolution. It is an optional document, i.e. gminas (communes) do not have the obligation to draw it up. The local plan shall mandatory include, among others: - use of land and the lines dividing areas for various land use; - the principles of environment, nature and cultural landscape protection; - the principles of cultural heritage, monuments and contemporary culture goods protection; - building lines, overall dimension of objects and indicators of build-up intensity; - the principles of modernization, expansion and building of communication systems and technical infrastructure. The consequence of the preparation of the local plan is an obligation to draw up documents such as economic impact forecast for adoption of local spatial management plan and environmental impact assessment.The solutions proposed in the plan may in fact significantly affect the transformation of the natural environment and the value of the properties. According to the law the content of the plan is divided into mandatory and optional part. The project of the plan is a subject of public consultations, requires a range of opinions and agreements to which act refers to. The geodetic area is the surface area of units of territorial division of the State according to the National Register of Boundaries.

metadata.name: 16.D.1 Percentage of individuals using the Internet for contacts with public authorities or public services for submitting completed forms
metadata.goal: Goal 16. Peace and justice - strong institutions
metadata.priority: Development of digitalization and information and communication technology in building effective public institutions, within the framework of e-administration and digitalization
metadata.definition: Percentage of disabled individuals using the Internet for private contacts with public authorities (in the last 12 months).
metadata.methodology: Data comes from the survey “Information society indicators”, which is a questionnaire based sample survey carried out with use of a face-to face interview, in which participation is voluntary. The survey is conducted in April every year. In accordance with the requirements set up in the European Commission regulations, the survey covers households in the whole country (with at least one person aged 16-74) and all persons aged 16-74 living in those households. The survey excludes persons living in collective accommodation establishments such as student dormitories, employee hostels, social welfare homes, convents, hospitals, barracks, prisons etc. Foreigners may participate in the survey as long as they be able to speak Polish.The term of “public authorities” refers to authorities providing public services, as well as conducting typical administrative activities. This includes, for example, the tax authorities, customs authorities, registration authorities, social security institutions, public health authorities (eg. the National Health Fund, hospitals), institutions of environmental protection, and other authorities  providing  public activities. There are local, regional, and national public authorities.Contact with the public authorities via the Internet includes for example: search for information on web pages, downloading official forms, sending filled forms (excluding own-written e-mails).

metadata.name: 16.D.2 Percentage of companies receiving orders via computer networks
metadata.goal: Goal 16. Peace and justice - strong institutions
metadata.priority: Development of digitalization and information and communication technology in building effective public institutions, within the framework of e-administration and digitalization
metadata.definition: Share of the number of enterprises carrying out e-sale through websites or EDI (Electronic Data Interchange) messages in the total number of active enterprises.
metadata.methodology: Sale via websites includes on-line orders placed in on-line stores or through electronic forms placed on a website or extranet regardless of the way of access (e.g. personal computer, laptop, mobile phone).Sale via EDI system is performed by messages of EDI type enabling creation of conditions for sending or receiving business information in an agreed format which allows its automatic processing e.g. EDIFACT, UBL, XML.Electronic Data Interchange is understood as sending or/and receiving messages (e.g. orders, invoices, payment transactions, product descriptions, transport documents, tax declarations) where: - it is carried out with enterprises, public administration authorities, financial institutions in agreed or standardized format which allows automatic processing e.g. EDI, EDIFACT, ODETTE, TRADACOMS, XML, xCBL, cXML, ebXML; - it excludes manually entered messages sent by electronic mail; - it is carried out through computer networks.Orders made by e-mail, fax or phone are excluded from e-trade transactions.

metadata.name: 16.D.3 Percentage of non-financial sector enterprises with broadband Internet access
metadata.goal: Goal 16. Peace and justice - strong institutions
metadata.priority: Development of digitalization and information and communication technology in building effective public institutions, within the framework of e-administration and digitalization
metadata.definition: Share of enterprises with broadband internet access in the total number of enterprises in the non-financial sector.
metadata.methodology: Data concern the enterprises employing more than 9 people, belonging to the PKD section: C,D,E,F,G,H,I,J,L,M (for data until the 2020 - only divisions 69-74), N divisions 77-82, S group 95.1.Broadband Internet access means access to a fixed connection with a bandwidth of at least 30Mbps.
